title: Tanker report - Week 20
summary:
VLCCs
Charterers have continued to hold the upper hand this week in the Middle East Gulf. The market for 270,000 tonnes going to China is hovering in the mid WS 50s and a charterer able to take newbuilding tonnage fixed at WS 46 for a run to South Korea. S-Oil also paid WS 46 for 274,000 tonnes to Onsan on the 2001 built DHT Utah which only has a bunker sire.

children.title: VLCCs
children.summary: Charterers have continued to hold the upper hand this week in the Middle East Gulf. The market for 270,000 tonnes going to China is hovering in the mid WS 50s and a charterer able to take newbuilding tonnage fixed at WS 46 for a run to South Korea.

children.title: Suezmaxes
children.summary: A healthy supply of tonnage in West Africa has seen rates stuck in the low WS 70s for Europe, although owners did manage to push rates up marginally from WS 72.5 to WS 73.75 while a short run to Spain is said to have been fixed by Repsol at WS 75. In the Black Sea there is very much a sense of déjà vu with rates for 135,000 tonnes unchanged at WS 85.

children.title: Aframaxes
children.summary: In the Mediterranean, a busy week led to a clear out of tonnage and with a couple of replacement cargoes thrown into the equation, owners managed to push the market up around WS 30 points to WS 135/137.5 level for both Med and Black Sea loading. However, with the improved market here, brokers feel there will be increased interest from suezmaxes on part cargoes thus capping the rates here.

children.title: Panamaxes
children.summary: It has been an uneventful week in the 55,000 tonnes ARA to US Gulf trade, with rates largely unchanged at WS 112.5 and Skikda rates at similar levels.

children.title: Clean
children.summary: It has been a week to forget for owners in the Middle East Gulf, with the market for 75,000 tonnes to Japan losing around WS 17.5 points to WS 90 and there is even talk of a ship being on subjects at WS 85. It is a similar story in the 55,000 tonnes AG/Japan trade where rates remain under pressure with the market losing almost 15 points to sit now at around WS 97.5 .
